original: Anthony compares a returning brother to a shipwrecked boat that has reached shore.
english: Do not throw back into the sea what barely survived enough to reach land.
spanish: No devuelvas al mar lo que apenas sobrevivió hasta tocar tierra.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Anthony 21
notes: Mode A queue from digitized Ward PDF, Anthony 21, p. 5. Short working rendering; copyright-gated, avoid long translated excerpts.

original: Anthony distinguishes natural movement, movement from excess, and movement from demonic assault.
english: Not every disturbance has the same root; discern the movement before fighting it.
spanish: No toda turbación tiene la misma raíz; distingue el movimiento antes de combatirlo.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Anthony 22
notes: Mode A queue from digitized Ward PDF, Anthony 22, p. 5. Short working rendering; copyright-gated.

artifact: Anthony 23 — The Burden Each Age Can Bear
original: Anthony says the same warfare is not given to every generation.
english: Mercy measures the trial to the strength that can bear it.
spanish: La misericordia mide la prueba según la fuerza que puede soportarla.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Anthony 23
notes: Mode A queue from digitized Ward PDF, Anthony 23, p. 5. Short working rendering.

artifact: Anthony 24 — The Doctor in the City
original: Anthony learns of a doctor in the city who gives his surplus to the poor and sings daily with the angels.
english: The desert is not the only place where hidden holiness keeps watch.
spanish: El desierto no es el único lugar donde vela una santidad escondida.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Anthony 24
notes: Mode A queue from digitized Ward PDF, Anthony 24, p. 5. Short working rendering.

original: Anthony says people will call the sane one mad because he is unlike them.
english: When the crowd becomes fevered, clarity itself is accused of illness.
spanish: Cuando la multitud enferma, la claridad misma es acusada de enfermedad.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Anthony 25
notes: Mode A queue from digitized Ward PDF, Anthony 25, p. 5. Short working rendering.

original: Anthony withdraws to pray for understanding of a passage from Leviticus.
english: When interpretation becomes pride, go far enough away to ask for a teacher.
spanish: Cuando la interpretación se vuelve orgullo, aléjate lo bastante para pedir un maestro.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Anthony 26
notes: Mode A queue from digitized Ward PDF, Anthony 26, p. 5. Short working rendering.

original: Amoun asks why Anthony is better known despite Amoun's stricter rule.
english: Strictness does not make the name luminous; love does.
spanish: La severidad no vuelve luminoso el nombre; el amor sí.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Amoun of Nitria 1
notes: Mode A queue from digitized Ward PDF, Amoun of Nitria 1, p. 27. Short working rendering.

original: Poemen tells Amoun that if he cannot be silent, sayings of the Fathers are less dangerous than Scripture talk.
english: When silence fails, speak from tested humility before speaking from authority.
spanish: Cuando falla el silencio, habla desde la humildad probada antes que desde la autoridad.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Amoun of Nitria 2
notes: Mode A queue from digitized Ward PDF, Amoun of Nitria 2, p. 27. Short working rendering.

original: Amoun gives a brother a short prayer to use when temptation comes.
english: At the hour of danger, a small prayer can become a road back to the desert.
spanish: En la hora del peligro, una oración pequeña puede volverse camino de regreso al desierto.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Amoun of Nitria 3
notes: Mode A queue from digitized Ward PDF, Amoun of Nitria 3, p. 27. Short working rendering.

artifact: John the Dwarf 1 — Fruit of Obedience
original: John waters dry wood until it bears fruit.
english: Obedience can water dead wood until patience itself bears fruit.
spanish: La obediencia puede regar madera muerta hasta que la paciencia misma dé fruto.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, John the Dwarf 1
notes: Mode A queue from digitized Ward PDF, John the Dwarf 1, p. 73. Short working rendering.

artifact: John the Dwarf 2 — Angels and Work
original: John tries to live like the angels, then learns he is human and must work to eat.
english: Wanting angelic freedom does not cancel the human need to work and eat.
spanish: Querer la libertad de los ángeles no cancela la necesidad humana de trabajar y comer.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, John the Dwarf 2
notes: Mode A queue from digitized Ward PDF, John the Dwarf 2, p. 73. Short working rendering.

artifact: John the Dwarf 3 — Starve the Enemy City
original: John compares conquest of an enemy city to cutting off its water and food.
english: What is not fed soon loses the strength to rule you.
spanish: Lo que no se alimenta pronto pierde la fuerza para gobernarte.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, John the Dwarf 3
notes: Mode A queue from digitized Ward PDF, John the Dwarf 3, p. 73. Short working rendering.
